files_modified: apps/api/src/api/utils/domain_affinity.py (NEW — shared module, single source of truth)
apps/api/src/api/routes/inbox.py (added _apply_domain_affinity_enrichment helper + 2 call sites: main route + filtered route, post-items pre-return)
scripts/run_domain_aware_matching_audit.py (refactored to import STRONG_SIGNALS + ADJACENCY + domain_affinity from new module — single source of truth)

runtime_inference.rationale: Audit showed strong signals alone yield 100% expected_vs_inferred match rate; no DB query at request time.
runtime_inference.fallback: If no strong signal present, cv_domain='other' and all items get neutral affinity.

shared_module_constants.STRONG_SIGNALS: 10 domains × ~80 canonical IDs (data, finance, hr, marketing, sales, supply, engineering, operations, legal, admin)
shared_module_constants.WEAK_SIGNALS: kept local to audit script only — runtime uses strong-only

validation.matching_unchanged: 39 scoring/matching tests passed — score, order, offer ids identical to pre-patch
validation.inbox_route_smoke: 200 OK on /api/inbox with profile_01_data_analyst — 5 returned items all carry domain_affinity (aligned/adjacent) and domain_affinity_score (2/1)
validation.audit_script_refactor: 100% expected_vs_inferred match rate preserved after import refactor (same numbers as pre-refactor)
validation.schema_validation: InboxItem rejects bogus domain_affinity values (pydantic pattern enforcement)

last_domain_aware_soft_signal_v1.frozen_rule: Domain affinity added as soft signal only (aligned/adjacent/distant); no impact on scoring or filtering. Pure enrichment layer — matching behavior strictly identical.
non_regression: exception in enrichment is swallowed and logged — never breaks /inbox response

v1_1_to_db_mapping_inside_audit.consulting_strategy: operations (proxy — no consulting tag in DB)

before_after_binary_vs_3_level.interpretation: shifting 25 pp of offers from hard-excluded to soft-kept (adjacent)

adjacencies_validated_correct: data ↔ finance (Contrôleur de gestion catches data analysts in finance dept)
finance ↔ operations (CFO/financier titles often mistagged operations — adjacency saves)
sales ↔ marketing (Growth/Account Manager, Digital Account Manager, User Acquisition)
data ↔ marketing (Business Analyst MOA, Marketing Analyst)
hr ↔ admin (Finance Transformation Administrator pulled in for HR)
adjacencies_too_noisy_in_db_11_tag: data ↔ engineering — catches all hard mechanical/aero/composite engineering (engineering_software needed but DB collapses both)
supply ↔ engineering — same problem (supply only wants industrial process, gets all engineering)
hr ↔ operations — operations is overly broad in 11-tag DB (catches HSE, Automation Engineer, Construction PM)
sales ↔ operations — same problem (catches HSE, Automation Engineer, CFO)
missing_adjacencies_observed_in_distant_: sales ↔ supply (Technico-Commercial, Account Manager Villa Supply have hybrid sales+supply nature)
engineering ↔ data already present but does not help in reverse for industrial profiles

last_domain_affinity_audit_v1.decision_rationale: Quantitatively very useful (-25.3 pp exclusion vs binary, soft-keep 37.5% avg). Qualitatively mixed: 5 adjacency pairs validated correct, 4 pairs too noisy due to 11-tag DB collapsing engineering_software+engineering_industrial and over-broad operations. Recommend: when activated as runtime soft signal, weight adjacent < aligned (re-rank only, never hard exclude). Tighten v2 noisy pairs after DB taxonomy refinement (split engineering subdomains).
last_domain_affinity_audit_v1.frozen_rule: Domain affinity is a soft re-rank signal only; never a hard filter as long as DB taxonomy uses single engineering and broad operations tags.
artifacts_produced: scripts/run_domain_aware_matching_audit.py (modified — ADJACENCY set + domain_affinity helper + per-profile affinity_projection + 3-level samples)
docs/ai/reports/domain_aware_matching_audit_v1.md (regenerated with affinity matrix + per-profile adjacent/distant samples)
non_regression: domain-aware matching still NOT enabled in production

rules: strong_signal in curated per-domain set adds +3.0 to that domain
data-driven distribution from offer_skills x offer_domain_enrichment still contributes (downweighted x0.2 for weak signals)
require >=1 strong signal; otherwise cv_domain = 'other' (low confidence)

average_hard_filter_exclusion_pct.delta: +0.6 (P1 now correctly maps to data, which has fewer offers than finance)

average_aligned_pct.delta: -0.7 (more truthful: P1 aligned drops from 107 finance offers to 65 data offers)

fixed_mismatches.root_cause: BF data-driven map biased: skill:business_intelligence top=finance (45/116), skill:excel top=finance (11/27), and skill:sql/statistical_programming/data_visualization had 0 BF signal

remaining_edge_cases: P1 hard exclusion 91.2% high — data offers are scarce (~65/876) in BF; ratio reflects catalog reality, not inference bug
P4 marketing has only 20 aligned offers in BF (small target market)
skill:compliance is treated as weak (would otherwise drag P3 finance toward engineering); may need re-evaluation if compliance officer profile is ever audited
non_regression: domain-aware matching still NOT enabled in production

last_domain_aware_matching_audit_v1_base.note: Replaced by last_cv_domain_inference_hardening_v1 above; original audit metrics preserved in before_after_metrics.

method.skill_to_domain_map: aggregated from offer_skills JOIN offer_domain_enrichment grouped by canonical_id, domain_tag
method.cv_domain_inference: per-skill normalized domain distribution, summed across CV canonical_ids, top domain wins
filter_projections: hard_filter: drop mismatched, count would_keep / would_exclude / exclusion_pct
soft_filter: low priority for mismatched, neutral kept, high for aligned

expected_vs_inferred_mismatches: P1_data_analyst expected=data inferred=finance (excel/sql/data_analysis dominate finance domain in BF dataset)

decision_criteria_for_next_sprint.if_hard_filter_exclusion_high_and_mismat: hard filter may be safe to ship
decision_criteria_for_next_sprint.if_expected_vs_inferred_lt_80_pct: strengthen cv_domain inference (more skills, smarter weighting) before any runtime activation

moustapha.reason: IA1 improved BF-242343 score 41 to 47, matched_core 1 to 2, missing_core 1 to 0; top offer and ranking unchanged.

last_ai1_prompt_preserve_content_v1.goal: prevent IA1 rebuilt_profile_text from compressing or summarizing CV content before deterministic parsing
rules_added: do not freely rewrite, compress, summarize, or elegantly paraphrase
line-by-line or block-by-block reconstruction when useful
do not convert detailed missions into short abstract summary
do not remove repeated-looking content unless exact extraction artifact

last_ai1_off_on_real_provider_comparison.global_observation: IA1 helps noisy long CV but degrades a clean CV when rebuilt_profile_text replaces deterministic input directly.

last_ai1_cvtest_comparison.global_observation: IA1 improves some poorly segmented CVs but is not safe as unconditional working_cv_text replacement; best next decision is dirty-CV-only activation or merge policy.
